metric_value: Windows-only automated testing; differentiated from cross-platform competitors
notes: SQA chose Windows-focused niche vs competitors with Unix/cross-platform approaches

metric_value: Server-based repository integrates all 4 testing tools; enables shared scripts and historical analysis
notes: Central repository was SQA's key differentiator; enabled cross-tool data sharing

metric_value: SQA Suite uniquely able to test OLE-enabled components and ActiveX objects in Windows environment
notes: Key differentiation from competitors at time of study

metric_value: Shortened from 2-3 years to less than 1 year due to 4GL tools
notes: Context for why testing tools became critical - faster cycles left less time for manual testing

metric_value: Aberdeen predicts every technology buyer will consider automated testing tools within two years
notes: Strong prediction of market demand for automated testing becoming universal requirement

metric_value: Automated testing tools became standard by 1998-2000; entire category grew substantially; CI/CD practices embedded by 2005
notes: Aberdeen's prediction proved accurate; automated testing is now universal DevOps practice

metric_value: Positioned as serious contender in testing arena; aggressive product enhancement schedule
notes: Aberdeen assessed SQA as having strong market position

metric_value: Acquired by Rational Software in late 1996/early 1997 for ~$300M; viability confirmed by premium acquisition
notes: Rational-SQA merger announced November 1996; valued SQA at ~$300M stock transaction

metric_value: Tool must withstand rigorous trials with immature code to gather maximum information during development
notes: First of six evaluation criteria; SQA rated positively

metric_value: Support for testing very large applications and load testing capacity
notes: SQA LoadTest component addressed this criterion

metric_value: Ability to handle changes in test scripts during execution and leverage existing scripts
notes: Dynamic script modification capability for rapidly changing applications

metric_value: Ability to integrate with third-party tools and adapt to multiple development environments
notes: Repository-based approach enabled third-party integration

metric_value: Integrated defect management with priority assignment and resolution tracking
notes: SQA Manager and SQA Process components delivered defect lifecycle management

metric_value: Comprehensive test data reporting with historical analysis capability
notes: Repository enabled historical trend analysis of defects

metric_name: Windows NT Platform Adoption for Development
metric_value: Enterprise IS migrating development to Windows NT; 32-bit testing becoming mandatory
notes: Migration to NT platform driving need for 32-bit testing tools

metric_name: SQA Suite vs Competitors: 16/32-bit Script Reuse
metric_value: SQA uniquely leverages test scripts across both 16-bit and 32-bit without modification
notes: Key differentiator during Windows 16-to-32-bit migration period

metric_value: Cross-platform testing strategy with Unix and Windows support; WinRunner product
notes: Contrasted with SQA's Windows-only focus

metric_value: Aberdeen strongly suggests IS managers serious about delivering robust client-server applications evaluate SQA Suite
notes: Explicit Aberdeen buying recommendation - rare in their profile format

metric_value: Testing must be integral part of development cycle; without continuous testing apps will be more costly, delayed, buggier
notes: Foundational argument for shift-left testing philosophy now standard in DevOps

metric_value: Repository-based suite pricing; individual product or full suite purchase options
notes: Suite approach offered more value than individual tools
